notes: WIN-NAV-007. Pins exactly what the row's compiled-but-unverified gates already claim and no more: (a) definition.xml carries the two title-bar metrics height-window-title=18 / height-floating-title=14 and the <settings> mapping titleHeight -> @height-window-title / floatTitleHeight -> @height-floating-title; (b) the six frame-activation style slots (activeColor/activeBorderColor -> @primary, activeTextColor -> @on-primary, deactiveColor -> @disabled-container, deactiveTextColor -> @outline, deactiveBorderColor -> @outline-variant) per docs/design/05-navigation.md 7.1; (c) FileDefinitionWidgetDraw.cxx pushes these into StyleSettings generically (SetTitleHeight/SetFloatTitleHeight and the Set{Active,Deactive}{,Text,Border}Color setters) -- this is SHARED cross-control, cross-platform plumbing, NOT title-bar-specific Material source, so it proves the same generic behaviour on every platform, not something Windows-specific. The consumption half is deliberately pinned as an ABSENCE fact: nothing consumes these slots for an active/inactive visual distinction. brdwin.cxx (the only native drawer of a VCL-rendered floating/tearoff title bar) never calls GetActiveColor/GetDeactiveColor/GetActiveTextColor/GetDeactiveTextColor, and vcl/win/window/salframe.cxx never sets a DWM caption/border/text colour (only DWMWA_USE_IMMERSIVE_DARK_MODE), so Material never reaches the real OS-drawn top-level chrome either. consumption.status is 'not-wired' and those markers MUST stay absent: the day someone genuinely wires active/inactive rendering (a real, build-verifiable improvement) this checker fails closed and forces a coordinated registry+row update rather than a silent drift to a claimed pass. Source evidence only: no native build, title-bar pixels, or runtime active/inactive capture are claimed (runtime_verified false).

owner.note: Shared cross-control, cross-platform StyleSettings plumbing (not title-bar-specific and not Windows-specific). Pinning it proves the generic metric/colour push exists; it is not evidence of a title-bar-specific Material render.

consumption.note: docs/design/05-navigation.md 7.1/7.2/7.7. brdwin.cxx's ImplStdBorderWindowView::DrawWindow draws VCL floating/tearoff title bars from GetFaceColor()/GetButtonTextColor() only; mbDisplayActive triggers a repaint but is never read to select between active/deactive slot colours. The real top-level window chrome is DWM-owned and salframe.cxx sets no caption/border/text colour. So the frame-activation slots exist and are pushed into StyleSettings but nothing consumes them for an active/inactive visual distinction -- the absence markers below MUST stay absent from comment-stripped code so the row cannot silently drift to a claimed pass without a deliberate registry+row update the day it is actually wired.
